text: Artificial intelligence is changing how we think about creativity. Today, AI can generate paintings, compose music, and even write stories or poems. Some people worry that machines will replace human artists. However, AI lacks true emotion and personal experience. It works by learning patterns from existing works, not by feeling inspiration. Human creativity comes from emotions, mistakes, and unique life perspectives. AI can be a helpful tool—for example, a musician might use AI to suggest new melodies, or a writer may get ideas for a plot. The best results often come from combining human imagination with AI's speed and data. So, instead of replacing art, AI is becoming a new creative partner.
